atoms.content: Python is an interpreted, high-level programming language that prioritizes readability, using whitespace (indentation) to define code blocks instead of curly braces.
Python reads like plain English. Instead of assembling a complex machine with tiny gears (like C++), you give high-level instructions to a smart assistant.

1. Indentation matters.
2. No semicolons needed.
3. Huge standard library.
Does Python use curly braces {} to define the scope of a function? (Answer: No, it uses indentation).
print() displays output to the screen. input() pauses the program and waits for the user to type something. These are the first functions every learner uses.
Comments are notes for humans that Python ignores. Use # for single-line comments and triple quotes (triple-double-quotes) for multi-line comments.
